data.title: EXCLUSIVE: Trinity Capital's New Loan Fuels Bobbie's Mission To Set New US Infant Nutrition Standards
data.description:
Alternative asset manager Trinity Capital Inc. (NASDAQ:TRIN) announced on Thursday that it has finalized a significant financing agreement with Bobbie, the mother-founded organic infant nutrition company.

The financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

This strategic capital infusion, designated for equipment and assets, will bolster Bobbie's production capacity at its Ohio facility, accelerating the company's national growth and ensuring broader distribution of its specialized products.

Bobbie, operating on a direct-to-consumer subscription model in the U.S., ensures families have reliable access to its transparent, clean nutrition.

Also Read: Alpha Buying: Private Credit Isn't the Villain Headlines Claim It Is

The brand, committed to simple, premium ingredients for healthy development, debuted a pioneering USDA-certified Organic whole milk infant formula in April 2025—the first of its kind manufactured domestically.

Ryan Little, Senior Managing Director of Equipment Finance at Trinity Capital, praised the collaboration: "Bobbie is addressing a clear need in the market by providing American families with a trustworthy, organic formula option produced right here in the U.S."

The investment directly supports Bobbie's primary goal: strengthening confidence in U.S.-made infant formula and reinforcing the domestic supply chain for this critical commodity.

Laura Modi, CEO and Founder of Bobbie, affirmed that the funding will allow them "to continue to do exactly that."

As the nation's first mom-founded formula brand, Modi stressed their obligation "to create the highest quality infant formula – one that we feed to our own babies."

Recent Financing

Last week, Trinity Capital announced a $130 million term loan facility with Candel Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:CADL).

This non-dilutive financing will significantly strengthen Candel's balance sheet, funding the initiation of a pivotal Phase 3 trial for its immunotherapy, CAN-2409, in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) in Q2 2026.

The capital will also accelerate pre-commercialization activities for CAN-2409 in prostate cancer.

Price Action: TRIN shares are trading 1.69% higher at $15 premarket at the last check on Thursday.

  • CoreWeave Is ‘Frightening… It's Unbelievable,' Says Expert, Expecting Its Revenue To Multiply Nearly 5x By 2028

Photo by Natee Meepian via Shutterstock

data.title: Meet Lightstone DIRECT: The Private Real Estate Investing Platform Raising the Bar for Individual Investors
data.description:
Lightstone has launched a direct real estate investing platform for accredited investors looking for a higher pedigree manager. . For investors who are facing an uncertain environment and looking for a partner that balances rigor with direct access, Lightstone DIRECT  is a compelling proposition with scale, transparency, and alignment that can be lacking on other platforms. 

Introducing Lightstone DIRECT

Lightstone is not new to real estate. The firm has been developing, owning, and operating property since 1986. The innovation is in the delivery model. Lightstone DIRECT is making this institutional machinery available to individuals, offering qualified investors the chance to sit at the same table that, until now, was effectively off-limits to all but giants like pension funds and endowments. The difference is about more than mere access, it’s about creating direct, meaningful relationships where investor and manager are truly aligned.

From Single Asset to $12 Billion Portfolio

Lightstone grew out of the investing success of  David Lichtenstein, a multi-billionaire and visionary real estate investor.  Over nearly four decades, the firm has institutionalized while maintaining a nimble approach and creative edge. Lightstone's portfolio is now valued at around $12 billion, with more than 25,000 residential units, 12 million square feet of industrial space, 3,100 hotel keys, retail, office, and life science properties. The breadth and national reach is paired with vertical integration – Lightstone controls all phases of the investment lifecycle:  acquisitions, development, operations, property management, and asset management are all under one roof.

Just as notable, Lightstone achieved this scale without significant LP participation l for most of its life. This autonomy kept the company nimble, as it weathered every real estate cycle since the 1980s such as the savings and loan crisis, the dot-com recession, the 2008 financial crisis, and the pandemic years. This cycle-tested approach, tested over decades, adds a level of practical wisdom and discipline not typically seen in the dispersed landscape of private real estate investments.

Renderings of 130 William, a Lightstone-developed luxury residential project in New York City

Why Real Estate, and Why Now?

Private-market real estate remains underweighted in individual portfolios, particularly compared to institutions that habitually allocate 10–20% of their portfolios to it. Why? Absence of trusted access, fee-heavy structures introduced by intermediaries, and misalignment risk between capital providers and operators. Many investment platforms that promised democratization through crowdfunding have instead disillusioned investors with superficial due diligence, overly optimistic projections, and multi-layered fee structures that can erode returns.

On the other hand, private real estate in the right hands has a proven track record of delivering risk-adjusted returns, durable income, and true diversification benefits. In spite of the economic uncertainties of today, the asset class is viewed by many as a buffer to volatility on account of its low correlation with public markets and its inflation-protective income streams.

Why is Lightstone Worth Considering?

In a real estate market dominated by generic funds and crowded platforms, Lightstone offers something refreshingly uncommon: direct, transparent access to deals that put investors side-by-side with the experts who built the portfolio.

Direct-to-Sponsor Access, No Middlemen

Lightstone DIRECT cuts out the middlemen and opacity of  crowdfunding sites and private fund structures. Investors deal directly with Lightstone, investing in the same distinct assets that Lightstone pursues with its own capital. The site provides a genuine partnership. A single deal is shared by all investors together with the firm’s principals, who have direct lines of communication and a flat, single-layered structure.

Co-Investment and Real Skin in the Game

Lightstone doesn’t just request external capital, it coinvests a minimum of 20% of the equity in every Lightstone DIRECT transaction.  This level of alignment is rare. Most managers only make a token co-investment. And, in the case of third-party syndication platforms, the platform itself typically makes zero co-investment. The Lightstone DIRECT model is an indication of confidence in the opportunity, Lightstone’s management would gain or lose directly alongside its investors.

Institutional Scale, Individual Access

With $12 billion in assets and decades  of operating history, Lightstone delivers the reach and scale of an institutional investor to individual partners. The portfolio spans multifamily housing, industrial warehouses, hospitality, and select high-growth markets backed by over 500 professionals nationwide. For new investors or veterans, this opens up exposure to quality, diversification, and sourcing capabilities few “direct” channels can offer.

Vertically Integrated Operations 

Sourcing and acquiring through developing and managing, Lightstone tightly controls all  elements of the investment process. This structure can reduce “fee leakage” and misaligned incentives that occur when there are too many hands on a project. Investors benefit from unfiltered feedback, direct communication to the decision-makers, and more transparency with regard to performance metrics, cash flows, and strategy.

Deal-by-Deal Option, Not Blind Pools

Rather than blind-pooling investor capital into a mix of assets, Lightstone DIRECT provides access to carefully selected single-asset opportunities. Investors can choose which opportunities to invest in, enhancing control and allowing more intentional, thoughtful allocation decisions without being locked into redemption timelines or over-diversified mega-funds that dilute performance.

Multifamily and Industrial Sector Focus

Today’s market is characterized by development pipelines tightening, ongoing demand for rentals, and growth in logistics, last-mile fulfillment, and other specialized industrial assets. Lightstone targets assets with the strongest fundamentals: multifamily in supply-constrained metros, warehouses benefiting from e-commerce and supply chain reconfiguration. Given Lightstone's breadth and scale, the platform has the potential to offer other timely CRE asset classes in the future.  . Lightstone DIRECT brings investors a diversified approach: responding to evolving market conditions while prioritizing sectors prized for stable cash flow and long-term resilience.

Real Human Partnership

At Lightstone, each investor is also assigned an experienced investor relations representative. No ticket queues or AI bots. No middleman between the investor and the ultimate sponsor of the deal. Lightstone, significantly co-invested alongside individual LPs, sees itself as a true steward of capital. 

Addressing the Pitfalls of Crowdfunding and Aggregators

Crowdfunding initially promised access but stumbled over execution. Opaque fee stacks, under-vetted sponsors, and a lack of post-investment stewardship became all too common, giving rise to high-profile scandals and calls for reform. Aggregator models, such as private REITs, offer diversification but do not bring the end investor the same level of control or transparency. 

  • Lightstone’s direct model bridges these deficiencies:
  • With a 20%+ coinvestment in each deal, Lightstone DIRECT investors can have confidence that Lightstone is staking considerable capital in the same deals. 
  • A strong track record is at the center. The company has weathered market shocks and realized a 25%+ IRR since 2004 on realized investments. 
  • With Lightstone’s in-house, hands-on methodology, investors have access in real time to property performance metrics, rental trends, and operational statistics, instead of summary reports from an arm’s-length third party.

Why Now? The Unique Timeliness of Lightstone's Launch

Market cycles ultimately separate the disciplined from the merely fortunate. After a long bull market and several moments of acute  dislocation, opportunity exists but experience and prudence matter more than ever. The current environment, with selective liquidity and a focus on disciplined underwriting, is exactly when this model may prove most valuable to individual investors –  alignment with a battle-tested team, with plenty of skin in the game and a track record that spans decades. 

By opening its doors to individual investors, with significant co-investment  in the same assets, Lightstone DIRECT introduces institutional-grade opportunity and discipline into a market hungry for both. 

For the more advanced investors looking to deploy their capital with transparency, control, and true partnership, Lightstone DIRECT deserves more than a glance. It’s a seat at a table that, until now, was reserved for the privileged few.

All investments involve risk. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Please visit lightstonedirect.com for a full set of disclosures.

data.title: Nvidia's New Partner And Rival: Intel, AMD Witness Improving Technical Indicators Amid Solid Momentum Gains
data.description:
The semiconductor trio, which includes Nvidia Corp.‘s (NASDAQ:NVDA) rival turned partner Intel Corp. (INTC) and its peer Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), have been riding a wave of momentum, signaling a potential shift in the tech landscape.

Two NVDA Rival Surge In Momentum Rankings

Technical indicators highlight AMD and INTC’s ascent. Benzinga Edge Stock Rankings reveal Intel's momentum at 90.21, up from 88.31, and AMD's at 93.41, up from 89.51, both showing positive short, medium, and long-term price trends.

However, Intel’s growth rankings remain poor at the 16.50th percentile.

On the other hand, AMD maintains robust growth and quality ranking, with a poor value score.

Additional performance details for both stocks are available here.

INTC And AMD Still Remain NVDA Peers Despite Developments

The partnership, cemented with NVDA’s $5 billion investment in Intel in September 2025, focuses on custom AI and PC solutions, integrating Intel's x86 CPUs with NVIDIA's NVLink and RTX GPUs.

This collaboration, hailed by NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang as a move from “rivalry to cooperation,” aims to dominate data centers and consumer markets.

On the other hand, despite Huang touting AMD and NVDA as “very different companies,” AMD remains its peer.

Intel's Arc GPUs and AMD's Radeon Instinct accelerators are vying against NVDA's dominance in AI and gaming, continuing to be its fierce competitors.

What Does Momentum Ranking Entail?

The momentum percentile, as defined by Benzinga Stock Edge Rankings methodology, reflects a stock’s relative strength based on price movement patterns and volatility compared to peers, offering an essential measure to track when evaluating shifts in investor sentiment and market trends.

Price Action

INTC was 0.49% lower in premarket on Thursday, whereas AMD fell by 0.19%.

On Thursday, the futures of the S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Nasdaq 100 indices were trading in a mixed manner

Meanwhile, on Wednesday, the S&P 500 index ended 0.53% lower at 6,699.40, whereas the Nasdaq 100 index fell 0.99% to 24,879.01. On the other hand, Dow Jones declined 0.71% to end at 46,590.41.

data.title: Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts Weigh In On 3 Tech And Telecom Stocks With Over 5% Dividend Yields
data.description:
During times of turbulence and uncertainty in the markets, many investors turn to dividend-yielding stocks. These are often companies that have high free cash flows and reward shareholders with a high dividend payout.

Benzinga readers can review the latest analyst takes on their favorite stocks by visiting Analyst Stock Ratings page. Traders can sort through Benzinga's extensive database of analyst ratings, including by analyst accuracy.

Below are the ratings of the most accurate analysts for three high-yielding stocks in the communication services sector.

AT&T Inc (NYSE:T)

  • Dividend Yield: 4.34%
  • Wells Fargo analyst Eric Luebchow maintained an Overweight rating and cut the price target from $31 to $29 on Oct. 16, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 65%.
  • Goldman Sachs analyst Michael Ng assumed coverage on the stock with a Buy rating and a price target of $32 on Sept. 2, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 73%.
  • Recent News: On Oct. 22, the telecom giant reported operating revenues of $30.71 billion, representing a 1.6% increase compared to the same period last year, which fell short of the analyst consensus estimate of $30.87 billion.
  • Benzinga Pro’s real-time newsfeed alerted to latest T news.

Comcast Corp (NASDAQ:CMCSA)

  • Morgan Stanley analyst Benjamin Swinburne maintained an Equal-Weight rating and cut the price target from $38 to $35 on Oct. 7, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 74%
  • Recent News: On Sept. 29, Comcast named Michael J. Cavanagh as co-CEO, effective Jan. 2026.

Shutterstock Inc (NASDAQ:SSTK)

data.title: QuantumScape CEO Says Volkswagen Partnership Paves Way For Solid-State Car By 2030
data.description:
QuantumScape Corp. (NYSE:QS) Siva Sivaram says that the company's PowerCo partnership with Volkswagen AG (OTC:VWAGY) will help the company develop solid-state batteries for cars in the future.

Check out the current price of QS here.

Solid-state batteries use a solid electrolyte instead of a liquid-based solution, resulting in a more portable form factor and tighter packaging. Solid-state batteries could also provide better safety and faster charging speeds when compared to regular liquid-based batteries.

QuantumScape Targeting A Series Production Car By 2030

Speaking to investors and analysts at Wednesday's earnings conference, Sivaram highlighted that the company's QSE-5 solid-state battery, which was demonstrated at IAA mobility in Munich, powering a Ducati motorcycle, was "an emotional moment for all of us," as well as "a huge milestone for all of our employees, investors, and partners," Sivaram said.

"We are working toward automotive-grade standards with the goal of a series production car with QuantumScape Corporation technology before the end of the decade," Sivaram said.

Partnerships With Corning And Murata To Aid Ceramic Cell Separators

Sivaram also highlighted that agreements with Murata Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (OTC:MRAAF) (OTC:MRAAY) and Corning Inc. (NYSE:GLW) will help develop the company's solid-state batteries, which utilize ceramic cell separators, a key element in the solid-state battery architecture developed using QuantumScape’s COBRA process.

According to QuamtumScape, the COBRA process offers a 25 times improvement in heat treatment speed and occupies a fraction of the physical space per film start. It is also designed to enable faster, more efficient production with reduced equipment footprint.

"Our strategy involves partnering with specialized high-precision ceramic manufacturers such as Murata and Corning to scale up separator production," Sivaram said, adding that the companies would supply Ceramic separators to PowerCo, which would "handle final assembly."

QuantumScape's Earnings, China's Solid-State Breakthroughs

The news comes as QuantumScape announced its Q3 earnings, beating market consensus as it reported a loss of 18 cents per share, as compared to estimates of 21 cents per share. QuantumScape also announced that it ended the quarter with $1.08 billion in total liquidity.

Meanwhile, researchers in China have reportedly made multiple breakthroughs in developing solid-state battery technology, with one group of researchers developing a solid-state battery that could offer more than 600 miles of range and weigh at 220 lbs.

QuantumScape scores well on the Momentum metric and offers a favorable price trend in the Short, Medium and Long term. For more such insights, sign up for Benzinga Edge Stock Rankings today!

Check out more of Benzinga's Future Of Mobility coverage by following this link.
